risks: activeChangeRepointTools is local to index.ts and behavior-covered by tests, not exported for a direct static allow-list assertion; future additions should add behavior tests for each read/write class.

Some listed active-work tools derive change ownership from taskId or report payload rather than a top-level changeId; current hook only re-points when args.changeId is present, so future SC2 expansions should include schema-realistic mutator coverage before relying on those tools for pointer setup.

proposal.approval_evidence: User reported Warp tab title is overwritten by inspected ADV change IDs. Proposal persisted with problem, affected files, constraints, and validation plan.

discovery.approval_evidence: Discovery evidence: broad `args.changeId` hook in `plugin/src/index.ts`; title propagation through `events/status.ts`; existing test expected read tool `adv_task_list` to re-point; project wisdom confirms Warp/OpenCode title identity is title-update owned. Contract minted from agreement with AC/SC/C/DONT items.

design.approval_evidence: Independent validator assessed explicit allow-list as safer than broad `args.changeId` and aligned with structural correctness. Validator report persistence hit `WorkflowNotFoundError` from subagent context, but returned inline evidence. Design choice: explicit active-work mutator allow-list before reachability; read-only tools including `adv_change_show`, `adv_gate_status`, and `adv_task_list` do not re-point per user symptom/AC. Existing create/close/archive/forget dedicated hooks remain unchanged.

items.text: Checking another change’s status does not rename the current Warp tab; observable signal: `getStatus().activeChangeId` remains unchanged after read-only `tool.execute.before` calls.
Actual work on a change still sets the active pointer/title; observable signal: `getStatus().activeChangeId` becomes a reachable target change for an allowed work mutator.
Given no active pointer, when `tool.execute.before` runs for `adv_change_show` with a reachable `changeId`, then `getStatus().activeChangeId` remains `null`.
Given active pointer `activeA`, when `tool.execute.before` runs for `adv_gate_status` with reachable `changeId` `otherB`, then `getStatus().activeChangeId` remains `activeA`.
Given active pointer `activeA`, when `tool.execute.before` runs for `adv_task_list` with reachable `changeId` `otherB`, then `getStatus().activeChangeId` remains `activeA`.
Given no active pointer, when `tool.execute.before` runs for `adv_task_update` with reachable `changeId`, then `getStatus().activeChangeId` becomes that `changeId`.
Given `target_path` is present, when an allowed work mutator runs with `changeId`, then pointer remains unchanged.
The targeted active-change-pointer Vitest file exits with code 0.
Pointer correctness must use an explicit allow-list of active-work tool names, not suffix/name heuristics.
Existing `adv_change_forget` mismatch validation and bypass behavior must remain unchanged.
Existing reachable-change gate must remain in the allowed re-point path.
Do not change terminal title format in `terminal.ts` unless required by tests.
Do not re-point for read-only/status tools such as `adv_change_show`, `adv_gate_status`, or `adv_task_list`.

documents.proposal:
# Fix tab title repoint

## Why

ADV currently updates the Warp/terminal tab title to any reachable `changeId` passed to an ADV tool. That is correct when the agent is actively working on that change, but wrong for status/read/cleanup workflows that merely inspect another change. Those reads overwrite the tab title with an unrelated change ID and erase the user’s existing project/emoji context.

## What Changes

- Narrow session active-change pointer re-pointing in `plugin/src/index.ts` from “any tool with `args.changeId`” to an explicit active-work allow-list.
- Keep read-only/status/cleanup tools from mutating the session pointer/title.
- Preserve existing protections:
  - `target_path` cross-project calls do not touch caller pointer.
  - `adv_change_forget` keeps mismatch validation and bypass behavior.
  - reachable-change gate still protects allowed repoints.

## User Outcomes

- [ ] Checking another change’s status does not rename the current Warp tab.
- [ ] Cleanup/review workflows that inspect many changes do not churn the tab title.
- [ ] Actual work on a change still sets the tab title to that change ID.

## Affected Code

- `plugin/src/index.ts` — active-change pointer re-point predicate.
- `plugin/src/__tests__/active-change-pointer.test.ts` — regression coverage for read-only calls and mutator calls.

## Constraints

- Use structural allow-list, not heuristic parsing of tool names.
- Do not weaken phantom pointer reachability checks.
- Do not change terminal title formatting in `terminal.ts` unless tests prove necessary.

## Impact

No external API change. Runtime behavior changes only for ADV plugin session pointer/title updates.

## Risks

- Missing an active-work tool in the allow-list could keep title on previous/no change during real work.
- Adding a read-only tool to the allow-list would preserve the current bug.

## Validation Plan

- Add failing tests for read-only tools not re-pointing.
- Add/keep passing test for allowed mutator re-point.
- Run targeted Vitest for active-change pointer tests.
documents.problemStatement: ADV terminal/Warp tab title should represent the change currently being worked, not whichever change was most recently inspected. Read-only/status/cleanup ADV tools currently carry `changeId` and trigger the same active pointer update path as work tools, causing tab title churn and overwriting project emoji/title context.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement

## Success Criteria

- SC1: Checking another change’s status does not rename the current Warp tab; observable signal: `getStatus().activeChangeId` remains unchanged after read-only `tool.execute.before` calls.
- SC2: Actual work on a change still sets the active pointer/title; observable signal: `getStatus().activeChangeId` becomes a reachable target change for an allowed work mutator.

## Acceptance Criteria

- AC1: Given no active pointer, when `tool.execute.before` runs for `adv_change_show` with a reachable `changeId`, then `getStatus().activeChangeId` remains `null`.
- AC2: Given active pointer `activeA`, when `tool.execute.before` runs for `adv_gate_status` with reachable `changeId` `otherB`, then `getStatus().activeChangeId` remains `activeA`.
- AC3: Given active pointer `activeA`, when `tool.execute.before` runs for `adv_task_list` with reachable `changeId` `otherB`, then `getStatus().activeChangeId` remains `activeA`.
- AC4: Given no active pointer, when `tool.execute.before` runs for `adv_task_update` with reachable `changeId`, then `getStatus().activeChangeId` becomes that `changeId`.
- AC5: Given `target_path` is present, when an allowed work mutator runs with `changeId`, then pointer remains unchanged.
- AC6: The targeted active-change-pointer Vitest file exits with code 0.

## Constraints

- C1: Pointer correctness must use an explicit allow-list of active-work tool names, not suffix/name heuristics.
- C2: Existing `adv_change_forget` mismatch validation and bypass behavior must remain unchanged.
- C3: Existing reachable-change gate must remain in the allowed re-point path.

## Avoidances

- DONT1: Do not change terminal title format in `terminal.ts` unless required by tests.
- DONT2: Do not re-point for read-only/status tools such as `adv_change_show`, `adv_gate_status`, or `adv_task_list`.
documents.design:
Implementation approach:
- Replace the broad `if (args.changeId)` repoint gate in `plugin/src/index.ts` with a structural allow-list predicate for active-work ADV tools.
- Keep `adv_change_forget` early-return before the predicate.
- Keep `target_path`, same-pointer no-op, reachability gate, and debug logging behavior inside the allowed path.
- Update `plugin/src/__tests__/active-change-pointer.test.ts` to assert read-only tools (`adv_change_show`, `adv_gate_status`, `adv_task_list`) never re-point, while a representative mutator still does.
- Avoid heuristic inference from tool names like `*_show`; correctness owned by explicit allow-list.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary

## Outcome
ADV now keeps the terminal/Warp tab title tied to active work instead of read-only inspections. The delivered fix narrows pointer re-pointing to an explicit active-work tool allow-list while preserving reachability, cross-project, and forget protections.

## Verdict
APPROVED

## What Was Built
1. Added `activeChangeRepointTools` and `shouldRepointActiveChange` in `plugin/src/index.ts` so only active-work mutators can re-point the session active-change pointer.
2. Added regression coverage proving `adv_change_show`, `adv_gate_status`, and `adv_task_list` do not re-point on reachable inspected changes.
3. Updated mutator/reachability tests to use `adv_task_update` as the representative allowed work tool, including unreachable, disk fallback, and `target_path` cases.
4. Updated compaction/integration test setup that intentionally establishes active work to use the active-work mutator path.

## What Was Verified
- Verdict: APPROVED with 0 findings (0 blockers, 0 issues, 0 suggestions, 0 nits).
- Tests: RED tr_mqtwwiwi_288d7aa7 failed on the old read-only re-point bug; GREEN tr_mqtx3i3e_b66794ba passed targeted active-change pointer tests; VERIFY tr_mqtx5ha4 passed `pnpm run check`; tr_mqtx8ufh passed `bin/oc-test full`; tr_mqtx9cql passed `pnpm run build`.
- Review: independent adv-reviewer report `fixTabTitleRepoint|change:review:acceptance|adv-reviewer|1` returned READY with no findings; scanner bundle `fixTabTitleRepoint|change:scanner-bundle:review|adv-scanner-bundle|1` found no issues.
- Preview URL: not_applicable — backend/plugin hook and test-only change; no frontend, browser-visible, or visual-output surface. Task metadata `frontend=false` with backend/plugin rationale.
- Contract matrix: 13/13 required rows passed/respected; 0 failed, violated, unknown, or missing rows.

## Remaining Concerns
None.

clarify_findings.message: Proposal describes behavior that could fail but doesn't mention error handling or rollback. What happens when things go wrong?
